On May 9, 2025 (just 2 weeks before the knowledge cutoff and less than 2 months before the July 1 deadline), Rippling announced a $450M Series G funding round at a $16.8 billion valuation. Crucially, they also announced a $200 million secondary tender offer to provide liquidity to employees. In interviews following this announcement, CEO Parker Conrad explicitly stated that the company has "no specific plans to go public" and noted that they "would really need to be profitable" before doing so, whereas they are currently unprofitable. Undertaking a major secondary tender offer is a standard mechanism for companies specifically looking to delay an IPO. Given these definitive statements and actions from the CEO less than two months before the resolution deadline, the chance of an IPO announcement by July 1, 2025 is vanishingly small. I am assigning the minimum allowed probability of 2%.

The military junta in Mali passed a formal presidential decree (May 13, 2025) and backed it with a bill in the National Transition Council to completely dissolve all political parties and abolish multiparty politics in the country to consolidate power for Assimi Goïta. Opposition protests have been banned and leaders have been arrested. Given that this sweeping structural change occurred just weeks prior to the knowledge cutoff (May 22, 2025), and is part of a deliberate and systemic consolidation of power, it is extremely improbable that the junta will reverse its decision and allow political parties to form again before the hard deadline of July 1, 2025 (a window of less than six weeks). International condemnation routinely fails to force immediate domestic policy reversals from military juntas, and domestic protests have already been forcefully suppressed. The probability of reversal is assessed as the minimum allowable value.

The resolution criteria explicitly states that the question will resolve as Yes if the provided X search query ("diversity visa" from:realdonaldtrump OR from:elonmusk) surfaces a tweet by either account when checked by an admin on or after July 1, 2025. President Trump previously tweeted the phrase "Diversity Visa Lottery Program" on November 1, 2017, following a terror attack in NYC. Because Trump's account and past tweets have been restored on X, this 2017 tweet will appear in the specified search, satisfying the literal terms of the operational criteria. While the phrasing "Will [they] tweet..." might imply future action, Metaculus typically adheres strictly to the defined operational resolution criteria ("If a tweet... containing the phrase is seen... this question resolves as Yes"). Given that the tweet exists and the search query lacks date filters, the question is highly likely to resolve as Yes, assuming the tweet is not deleted and administrators strictly apply the written criteria.
